typecheck.command: cd "/Users/albertonunez/Documents/Windsurf/BurnBar" && xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: SessionDetailView still clears state too narrowly: the reset watches only session.sessionId, but the reload identity is provider + sessionId, so provider swaps can still leak prior conversation/summary/anchor state before the async refresh finishes.

ContextPackSessionDetailSurfaceTests still remain data-layer-only and never instantiate SessionDetailView or ContextPackSheet, so the contract's UI visibility/order/presentation wiring is not actually exercised.

ContextPackCrossFlowTests still bypass the real Dashboard -> SessionDetail -> ContextPackSheet path and do not assert exact ordered session-id equality, leaving same-anchor parity regressions unverified.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require SessionDetail stale-state clearing to key off the full reload identity (`provider + sessionId`, or an equivalent view identity scope) instead of `sessionId` alone; `.task(id:)` cancellation by itself is not enough.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fix-session review found that provider swaps can still leak prior conversation/summary/anchor state because the reset only watches `session.sessionId`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require cross-flow and Session Detail validation to instantiate the real DashboardView -> SessionDetailView -> ContextPackSheet path and assert exact ordered session-id parity, rejecting service-only helper coverage for contract-grade checks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The crossflow review found the tests still bypass real SwiftUI entrypoints and only approximate same-anchor parity with service-only helpers.
